section_title: 10 DETAILS OF THE UNDERLYING SCENARIOS USED TO CALCULATE THE IMPACTS

scenarios.title: A1 - raw material supply
scenarios.description: This module takes into account the extraction and processing of all raw materials and energy which occur upstream to the studied manufacturing process.

scenarios.title: A2 – transport to the manufacturer
scenarios.description: The raw materials are transported to the manufacturing site.

scenarios.title: manufacturing
scenarios.description: This module takes into account the production process.

scenarios.title: A4 – transport to the building site
scenarios.description:
Fuel type and consumption of vehicle or vehicle type used for transport: Truck 16-32 ton, 0,256 l diesel / km
Distance: 180 km
Capacity utilisation (including empty returns): 50%
Bulk density of transported products: Ecoinvent
Volume capacity utilisation factor: Ecoinvent

Kooltherm® K8 is only sold on a project basis. Kooltherm will therefore only be delivered directly to construction sites.

The following transport scenario applies:
– 100% directly to the construction site over 180 km with a 16-32 ton lorry (ecoinvent record: 'Transport, freight, lorry 16-32 metric ton, EURO5 {RER}| transport, freight, lorry 16-32 metric ton, EURO5 | Cut-off, U')

scenarios.title: A5 – installation in the building
scenarios.description:
At the construction site, packaging materials are released. Also 2% material losses have been taken into account

Parts of the installation quantity:
Material losses: 0,056 kg Kooltherm K8 - THE QUANTITY OF MATERIAL LOST DUE TO CUTTING IT IN THE RIGHT SHAPE
Packaging: 0,0135 kg PE foil, 0,0504 kg EPS Skids - THE PACKAGING WASTE AT THE CONSTRUCTION SITE

scenarios.title: C: End of life
scenarios.description:
The default scenario for synthetic isolation materials in the NBN/DTD B08-001 states 5% of the waste treatment consists of landfilling, the other 95% consists of incineration and/or energetic valorisation.

After incineration the aluminium will be recycled from the bottom ash (based on sustainability report of Indaver: samen werken aan een circulaire economie, duurzaamheidsrapport 2021).

The default scenario for metals (non-ferro) in the NBN/DTD B08-001 states 5% of the waste treatment consists of landfilling, the other 95% consists of recycling.

Module C2 – Transport to waste processing:
Truck 16-32 ton, 0,256 l diesel/km, Distance 30 km, Capacity utilisation 50%, Density of products: Ecoinvent scenario, Assumptions: Ecoinvent scenario
Truck 16-32 ton, 0,256 l diesel/km, Distance 50 km, Capacity utilisation 50%, Density of products: Ecoinvent scenario, Assumptions: Ecoinvent scenario
Truck 16-32 ton, 0,256 l diesel/km, Distance 10 km, Capacity utilisation 50%, Density of products: Ecoinvent scenario, Assumptions: Ecoinvent scenario

End-of-life modules – C3 and C4:
Wastes collected separately: 0
Wastes collected as mixed construction waste: 2,80 kg
Waste for re-use: 0
Waste for recycling: 0,09 kg
Waste for energy recovery: 2,57 kg
Waste for final disposal: 0,14 kg

scenarios.title: D – Benefits and loads beyond the system boundaries
scenarios.description:
QUANTITATIVE DESCRIPTION OF THE LOADS BEYOND THE SYSTEM BOUNDARIES: No loads

QUANTITATIVE DESCRIPTION OF THE BENEFITS BEYOND THE SYSTEM BOUNDARIES:
Avoided production of 13,4 MJ of heat using natural gas
Avoided production of 6,7 MJ of Belgian electricity mix
